title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Lynn discusses the late Gordon Croud with her mother, Adela Marchmont. Adela describes Gordon's widow, Rosaline (also known as Mrs. Andeh), as a neurotic and clumsy woman who suffered from trauma following an air raid. Rosaline is currently living in Frognack with her brother, David Hunter, an Irish man whom Adela describes as rude and impolite. Adela expresses suspicion regarding Rosaline’s motives, noting that she traveled from South America during the war, leading to rumors that she was seeking a wealthy husband.

Adela reveals that Gordon’s marriage to Rosaline invalidated his 1940 will, which had previously ensured the family's financial security. Consequently, Rosaline inherited everything. This development leaves Adela in financial distress; despite Gordon's previous promises to provide for the family, Adela is facing an overdrawn bank account and mounting bills for house repairs and war damage insurance. Lynn suggests that Rosaline has a moral obligation to help, but Adela refuses to ask for favors, especially given the potential influence of Rosaline's brother.

In a separate setting in Wormsley Valley, Francis Claude, a forty-eight-year-old woman known for her ability to afford and manage servants, observes her husband, Jeremy Claude. Jeremy, sixty-three, appears unusually expressionless during their meal, a change that Francis immediately notices. A fifteen-year-old girl serves them, appearing highly sensitive to Francis's reactions.
